source: Dream Physics: A Field Manual of the Sleeping Cosmos
description: Core theoretical concepts from the Dream Physics framework

concepts.name: Oneiros
concepts.definition: The raw experiential substance of dreams — the perceptible stuff from which images, sensations, emotions, identities, and narratives are formed.
properties: does not occupy space — it defines space
does not move through time — it becomes time
behaves like hybrid of sensory perception, emotional feeling, and narrative expectation

concepts.name: O-Node
concepts.definition: Any temporarily persistent dream-form whose boundaries appear stable to the dreamer. Stable crystallizations of Oneiros.
examples: a city that lasts several minutes
properties: tight whirlpools of meaning spinning inside perceptual fluid

concepts.name: Symbol Field
concepts.definition: A global, nonlocal shaping layer where collective myth, personal memory, archetype, and cultural imagery merge into probability densities.
concepts.mechanism: Symbolic Resonance — dream-mind attracts symbols that vibrationally match the emotional state being experienced

concepts.name: Affective Fields
concepts.definition: Gradient zones of dream reality formed by the intensity and coherence of emotion. Dream reality shaped by emotion the way spacetime is curved by mass.
concepts.equation: Scene Stability ∝ Emotional Coherence / Memory Fragmentation

concepts.name: Mnemonic Mass
concepts.definition: The gravitational pull exerted by emotionally-charged memories upon dream formation. Memory behaves as mass does.
properties: large memories distort dream-realities toward familiar narratives
stronger unresolved emotion = more mass = more warping

concepts.name: Memory Wells
concepts.definition: Stable gravitational basins of unresolved meaning in dream space, causing orbit around fixed storylines.

concepts.name: Identity Superposition
concepts.definition: The dream-self existing simultaneously as observer, participant, narrator, and sometimes multiple characters.

concepts.name: Symbolic Mechanics
concepts.definition: The physics of how symbols form, mutate, clash, hybridize, collapse into archetypes, and become narrative engines.

concepts.name: Kairotempics
concepts.definition: The physics of nonlinear dream time. Kairos (meaningful time) vs. Chronos (mechanical time) in the dream universe.

concepts.name: Dream Energetics
concepts.definition: What energy means in nonphysical worlds — emotional fuel dynamics, attention as currency, intention as force.
